title: Key by_state count maps by the State enum
description:
Key the `by_state` count maps by the `State` enum instead of `String` (report highest-leverage fix #3). Also removes the latent `Debug`-vs-serde divergence (`"inprogress"` vs `"in_progress"`).

## Sites
- `output.rs:727-733` — `DependencySummary.by_state` → `HashMap<State, usize>`.
- `snapshot.rs:56` — `IssuesInfo.states` → `HashMap<State, usize>`.
- Producers: `commands/graph.rs:200-222` and `snapshot.rs:274` → `entry(node.state)`.
- Consumer: `main.rs:2672` → `.get(&State::Done)`.

`State` is `Copy + Eq + Hash + Serialize(snake_case)`, so JSON stays valid.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `DependencySummary.by_state` and `IssuesInfo.states` are keyed by `State`, not `String`.
- [ ] Producers insert with the `State` value directly; no `format!("{:?}", state)` or `.to_string()` key construction remains.
- [ ] The `main.rs` consumer reads `State::Done` via the typed key.
- [ ] `--json` output for these maps serializes with the canonical snake_case state names (no `"inprogress"` regression); `cargo-ci` and `code-review` pass.
